source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Consider the multiplexor shown in <image>, where \( I_0, I_1, I_2, I_3 \) are four data input lines selected by two address line combinations \( A_1 A_0 = 00, 01, 10, 11 \) respectively, and \( f \) is the output of the multiplexor. EN is the Enable input.

The function \( f(x, y, z) \) implemented by the circuit shown in <image> is:
extraction_notes: image field contains an in-memory PIL image placeholder, not a path or URL

image_dependency: Question explicitly says 'multiplexor shown in the image' and asks for the function implemented by the shown circuit
Determining the answer depends on circuit wiring / selector-input relationships that are not fully specified in text alone
asset_registry_record shows image exists and registry_passed=true
multi_step: Requires interpreting a 4-to-1 multiplexor with address lines A1A0 selecting I0, I1, I2, I3
Need to read the circuit image to determine how x, y, z, and EN connect to the mux inputs/select lines
Then must derive the resulting Boolean function f(x,y,z) and match it to one of 4 choices
This is more than direct lookup and involves combined circuit interpretation plus logic simplification/comparison
verifiability: choices are provided, so the final answer is constrained and easy to check
answer_type=short_text with a concrete Boolean expression: 'x y z^{\prime}'
The provided answer exactly matches one of the listed options
registry_passed=true and answer_present=true, supporting reliable downstream checking

risk_flags: EN input may affect interpretation, but its exact active behavior is not stated in text
circuit details needed for solving are only available in the image

decision_reasons: Assets are complete: question, answer, and required image are all present, and registry_passed=true with no recorded issues.
Initial scores are strong, especially image_dependency=0.97 and verifiability=0.91, indicating a high-value image-grounded problem.
Although the circuit interpretation depends on the image and EN behavior is a minor interpretation risk, the core semantics are recoverable because the image asset exists.
